mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in INDPRO reflects improving real output momentum and rising operating leverage, causing higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the signal is evaluated each month.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth has a nonpositive walk-forward coefficient or Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, deteriorating labor-market slack reduces expected household income and cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or the downside hit rate is not above baseline by at least 5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Lower FEDFUNDS levels ease discount-rate pressure on equity cash flows and support risk appetite; a lower federal funds rate predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS below 0.25% is followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional median in more than 55% of triggered walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumption demand and near-term revenue momentum, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS 3-month growth does not produce a positive average 20-day excess return versus negative RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests with p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL growth combined with rising FEDFUNDS tightens financial conditions and compresses valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month growth crosses above 2% annualized and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation and rising-rate trigger has nonnegative average 20-day forward returns or its interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below 1000 signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 1000 is followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional median in more than 55% of triggered walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth paired with a falling UNRATE signals broad labor income expansion and lower recession risk, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through earnings-revision surprises.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down trigger has a 5-day forward hit rate below 52% or the interaction term is not positive in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, real demand expansion occurs without rate pressure, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth crosses above 0% and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if the positive-GDPC1 and low-FEDFUNDS regime produces 60-day S&P 500 returns below zero in more than 45% of triggered walk-forward observations.
